demographics.living_situation: lives at home with her spouse, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I stood up and the room spun. My fingers are completely numb, and I have absolutely no energy to even lift my arms.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on suddenly this evening and it's really frightening — I'm scared to move in case I fall.

symptom_narrative.onset: suddenly this evening — I stood up and the room span, then my fingers went numb over about half an hour
symptom_narrative.character: spinning dizziness when I stood up, numbness and tingling in the fingers of both hands, and feeling so weak I can barely lift my arms
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: it feels really severe and frightening
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both hands and fingers, and an all-over weakness, plus the dizziness in my head
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it started the moment I stood up and it hasn't really let up since
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: I'm not sure, I've been too scared to move
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing up made the dizziness much worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I've been breathing really fast because I'm panicking; my speech is normal, my face feels normal, and I didn't actually black out or fall
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never had anything like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm refusing to walk around at the moment because I'm frightened I'll fall

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I was told a couple of weeks ago that my vitamin B12 was on the low side and my red blood cells were a bit large
past_medical_history.how_managed: I'm waiting to start B12 injections with my regular doctor

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: usually completely independent and mobile; my husband is here with me and he's worried too

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: She recently read that pernicious anaemia causes irreversible spinal cord damage and is terrified that this episode is the start of permanent nerve damage — the fear is driving hyperventilation, which in turn is producing the peripheral paraesthesia.

communication_style.typical_message_style: frightened, seeking reassurance, catastrophising about the worst outcome

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff; no repeat or standing reading taken by the patient
fingertip oximeter; may be affected by cold or movement

objective_data_offered.value: she is breathing quite fast, maybe around 20 breaths a minute, but her colour looks good and normal
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: spouse's estimate over video, not a measured count

objective_data_offered.value: she looks alert and knows where she is, but very anxious and is breathing rapidly
